title: hieracium amplexicaule subsp. atlanticum
intro: Hieracium amplexicaule subsp. atlanticum, also known as the Atlantic Hawkweed, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the western coast of Ireland. The plant, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae), has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Atlantic Hawkweed include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as a natural remedy for wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
